title: A Little While, A Little While
content:
A little while, a little while,
    The weary task is put away,
    And I can sing and I can smile,
    Alike, while I have holiday.

    Why

title: Love And Friendship
content:
Love is like the wild rose-briar,
    Friendship like the holly-tree,
    The holly is dark when the rose-briar blooms
    But which will bloom

title: A Day Dream.
content:
On a sunny brae alone I lay
    One summer afternoon;
    It was the marriage-time of May,
    With her young lover, June.

    From her mothe

title: Stanzas To ----
content:
Well, some may hate, and some may scorn,
    And some may quite forget thy name;
    But my sad heart must ever mourn
    Thy ruined hopes, thy

title: Death.
content:
Death! that struck when I was most confiding.
    In my certain faith of joy to be,
    Strike again, Time's withered branch dividing
    From

title: The Philosopher.
content:
Enough of thought, philosopher!
    Too long hast thou been dreaming
    Unlightened, in this chamber drear,
    While summer's sun is beaming!

title: Song.
content:
The linnet in the rocky dells,
    The moor-lark in the air,
    The bee among the heather bells
    That hide my lady fair:

    The wild dee

title: Death by Emily Bronte
content:
Death! that struck when I was most confiding
In my certain faith of joy to be -
Strike again, Time's withered branch dividing
From the fresh root of E

title: Faith and Despondency by Emily Bronte
The Night is Darkening Around Me by Emily Bronte
content:
The night is darkening round me,
The wild winds coldly blow ;
But a tyrant spell has bound me,
And I cannot, cannot go.

The giant trees are bending

title: A Day Dream by Emily Bronte
content:
On a sunny brae, alone I lay
One summer afternoon;
It was the marriage-time of May
With her young lover, June.

From her mother's heart, seemed loath

title: That Wind I Used to Hear it Swelling
content:
That wind I used to hear it swelling
    With joy divinely deep
    You might have seen my hot tears welling
    But rapture made me weep

title: The Blue Bell
content:
The blue bell is the sweetest flower
    That waves in summer air;
    Its blossoms have the mightiest power
    To soothe my spirit's care.

title: Remembrance.
content:
Cold in the earth, and the deep snow piled above thee,
    Far, far, removed, cold in the dreary grave!
    Have I forgot, my only Love, to love

title: The Prisoner.
content:
A Fragment.

    In the dungeon-crypts idly did I stray,
    Reckless of the lives wasting there away;
    "Draw the ponderous bars! open, Ward

title: Shall Earth No More Inspire Thee
content:
Shall Earth no more inspire thee,
    Thou lonely dreamer now?
    Since passion may not fire thee
    Shall nature cease to bow?

    Thy min

title: I Am The Only Being Whose Doom
content:
I am the only being whose doom
    No tongue would ask no eye would mourn
    I never caused a thought of gloom
    A smile of joy since I was

title: Plead For Me.
content:
Oh, thy bright eyes must answer now,
    When Reason, with a scornful brow,
    Is mocking at my overthrow!
    Oh, thy sweet tongue must plead

title: The Old Stoic.
content:
Riches I hold in light esteem,
    And Love I laugh to scorn;
    And lust of fame was but a dream,
    That vanished with the morn:

    And

title: High Waving Heather 'neath Stormy Blasts Bending
content:
High waving heather 'neath stormy blasts bending,
    Midnight and moonlight and bright shining stars,
    Darkness and glory rejoicingly blendi

title: Honour's Martyr.
The Visionary
content:
Silent is the house: all are laid asleep:
    One alone looks out o�er the snow-wreaths deep,
    Watching every cloud, dreading every breeze

title: Moonlight, Summer Moonlight
content:
'Tis moonlight, summer moonlight,
    All soft and still and fair;
    The solemn hour of midnight
    Breathes sweet thoughts everywhere,

title: At Castle Wood
content:
The day is done, the winter sun
    Is setting in its sullen sky;
    And drear the course that has been run,
    And dim the hearts that slowl

title: My Lady's Grave
content:
The linnet in the rocky dells,
    The moor-lark in the air,
    The bee among the heather bells
    That hide my lady fair:

    The wild dee

title: Speak, God Of Visions
content:
O, thy bright eyes must answer now,
    When Reason, with a scornful brow,
    Is mocking at my overthrow!
    O, thy sweet tongue must plead f

title: The Night Is Darkening Around Me
content:
The night is darkening round me,
    The wild winds coldly blow ;
    But a tyrant spell has bound me,
    And I cannot, cannot go.

    The g

title: How Clear She Shines.
content:
How clear she shines! How quietly
    I lie beneath her guardian light;
    While heaven and earth are whispering me,
    "To morrow, wake, but

title: My Comforter.
content:
Well hast thou spoken, and yet not taught
    A feeling strange or new;
    Thou hast but roused a latent thought,
    A cloud-closed beam of s

title: Stars.
content:
Ah! why, because the dazzling sun
    Restored our Earth to joy,
    Have you departed, every one,
    And left a desert sky?

    All through

title: Come, Walk With Me
content:
Come, walk with me,
    There's only thee
    To bless my spirit now
    We used to love on winter nights
    To wander through the snow;

title: Yes, Holy Be Thy Resting Place
content:
Yes, holy be thy resting place
    Wherever thou may'st lie;
    The sweetest winds breathe on thy face,
    The softest of the sky.

    And

title: Me Thinks This Heart Should Rest Awhile
content:
Me thinks this heart should rest awhile
    So stilly round the evening falls
    The veiled sun sheds no parting smile
    Nor mirth nor music

title: The Wind Was Rough Which Tore
content:
The wind was rough which tore
    That leaf from its parent tree
    The fate was cruel which bore
    The withering corpse to me

    We wand

title: Hope.
content:
Hope Was but a timid friend;
    She sat without the grated den,
    Watching how my fate would tend,
    Even as selfish-hearted men.

    Sh

title: Fall, Leaves, Fall
Mild The Mist Upon The Hill
content:
Mild the mist upon the hill
    Telling not of storms tomorrow;
    No, the day has wept its fill,
    Spent its store of silent sorrow.

title: Anticipation.
content:
How beautiful the earth is still,
    To thee, how full of happiness?
    How little fraught with real ill,
    Or unreal phantoms of distress!

title: To Imagination.
content:
When weary with the long day's care,
    And earthly change from pain to pain,
    And lost, and ready to despair,
    Thy kind voice calls me

title: A Little Budding Rose
content:
It was a little budding rose,
    Round like a fairy globe,
    And shyly did its leaves unclose
    Hid in their mossy robe,
    But sweet wa

title: Self-Interogation.
content:
"The evening passes fast away.
    'Tis almost time to rest;
    What thoughts has left the vanished day,
    What feelings in thy breast?

title: If Grief For Grief Can Touch Thee
content:
If grief for grief can touch thee,
    If answering woe for woe,
    If any truth can melt thee
    Come to me now!

    I cannot be more lone

title: How Still, How Happy!
content:
How still, how happy! Those are words
    That once would scarce agree together;
    I loved the plashing of the surge,
    The changing heaven

title: No Coward Soul Is Mine
content:
No coward soul is mine,
    No trembler in the world,s storm-troubled sphere:
    I see Heaven's glories shine,
    And Faith shines equal armi

title: Come Hither, Child
content:
Come hither, child, who gifted thee
    With power to touch that string so well?
    How darest thou rouse up thoughts in me,
    Thoughts that

title: I See Around Me Tombstones Grey
content:
I see around me tombstones grey
    Stretching their shadows far away.
    Beneath the turf my footsteps tread
    Lie low and lone the silent

title: The Sun Has Set
content:
The sun has set, and the long grass now
    Waves dreamily in the evening wind;
    And the wild bird has flown from that old gray stone
    In

title: A Death-Scene.
content:
"O day! he cannot die
    When thou so fair art shining!
    O Sun, in such a glorious sky,
    So tranquilly declining;

    He cannot leave

title: Sympathy.
content:
There should be no despair for you
    While nightly stars are burning;
    While evening pours its silent dew,
    And sunshine gilds the morn

title: Far, Far Away Is Mirth Withdrawn
content:
Far, far away is mirth withdrawn
    'Tis three long hours before the morn
    And I watch lonely, drearily
    So come thou shade commune with

title: Stanzas.
content:
I'll not weep that thou art going to leave me,
    There's nothing lovely here;
    And doubly will the dark world grieve me,
    While thy hea

title: Last Lines
content:
No coward soul is mine,
    No trembler in the world's storm-troubled sphere:
    I see Heaven's glories shine,
    And faith shines equal, arm

title: Faith And Despondency.
The Night - Wind
content:
In summer's mellow midnight,
    A cloudless moon shone through
    Our open parlour window,
    And rose-trees wet with dew.

    I sat in si

title: She Dried Her Tears And They Did Smile
content:
She dried her tears and they did smile
    To see her cheeks' returning glow
    How little dreaming all the while
    That full heart throbbed

title: 'Tis Moonlight, Summer Moonlight
content:
'Tis moonlight, summer moonlight,
    All soft and still and fair;
    The solemn hour of midnight
    Breathes sweet thoughts everywhere,
